title: Server State vs Client State
body:
## Definition

**Server state** lives on the backend (users, posts) — async, shared, stale. **Client state** is UI-only (modal open, tab, draft text) — local and immediate.

## Why it exists

Different lifecycles and tools: don’t duplicate API data in Redux if a cache library handles it.

## How it works

Server: TanStack Query / SWR — fetch, cache, revalidate. Client: [useState](concept:use-state), Zustand, URL state.

## Common confusion

“Global state” is not all one bucket — auth profile (server) vs sidebar collapsed (client).

## In practice

Colocate server state in query hooks; keep global client state minimal.
bodyLinked:
## Definition

**[Server state](concept:server-state-vs-client-state)** lives on the backend (users, posts) — [async](concept:async-await), shared, stale. **[Client state](concept:server-state-vs-client-state)** is UI-only (modal open, tab, draft text) — local and immediate.

## Why it exists

Different lifecycles and tools: don’t duplicate API data in Redux if a cache library handles it.

## How it works

Server: [TanStack Query](concept:tanstack-query) / SWR — [fetch](concept:fetch), cache, revalidate. Client: [useState](concept:use-state), [Zustand](concept:zustand), URL state.

## Common confusion

“[Global state](concept:react-context-api)” is not all one bucket — auth profile (server) vs sidebar collapsed (client).

## In practice

Colocate [server state](concept:server-state-vs-client-state) in query hooks; keep global [client state](concept:server-state-vs-client-state) minimal.
